observed_preexisting: hooks/lib/allowlist.py (wildcard predicate at lines 521-523 already committed in becffc6 — not modified this cycle)

tasks_completed.changes: Added 21-line test method after test_op_mismatch_returns_none (line 633). Test writes sentinel with allowed_operations=[{"op":"Write"}] (no 'target' key), calls match_sentinel_grant_for_write with any target path, asserts result is not None and has no 'target' key.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause (aea75d9 / task 20260522-080646-B): entry_target=None fell through the match condition (None==target_path is False), causing CF-1 for all targetless grants. Fix committed in becffc6 at allowlist.py:521-523. This test proves the wildcard path is exercised.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: aea75d9 — chore(docs): ship cycle 20260522-000000 close-report and QA verification (prior TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite lacked wildcard test case)
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: The 5-method TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite class committed in becffc6 covered session mismatch, target mismatch, expired sentinel, and op mismatch — but not the targetless (wildcard) case where entry_target is None. The wildcard fix at allowlist.py:521-523 was committed without a test proving it.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: New test passes sentinel with no 'target' field and asserts match_sentinel_grant_for_write returns non-None, directly exercising the entry_target is None branch of the wildcard predicate.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: 21 lines: 7 lines docstring (root cause reference + wildcard predicate reference), 2 setup lines (task_id, session_id), 1 comment, 1 _write_sentinel call, 3 assertions (assertIsNotNone, assertEqual op, assertNotIn target), 4 try/finally lines, 2 blank lines. Docstring is substantive — references the exact root cause commit and line range for QA traceability. No removable lines.
